Aequer units have the square with tentacles over them. The large 1st box shows some numbers like 24x2 (which sometimes turn to 24x1) and 112 below 24x2. What do these numbers represent? Also there are sometimes tentacles in the small boxes (I had 3 of the 6 boxes with tentacles). What does tentacles in smaller boxes do? Normally at turn end they do an attack. Does filling all the small boxes with them do anything?
that's dmg at the end of the turn
some characters can give you more tentacles with their cards and exalts

All the boxes are the same they just indicate how many permanent tentacles you have, (You cannot go below one permanent tentacle), the 1st box is simply bigger because you click on it to access the tentacle stances for aequor
The number after the x just indicates how many times tentacles will attack at the end of your turn
And the amount of empty slots just shows you your max permanent tentacle count

Additionally the amount of times your tentacles attack is indicated separately with the number after x because the amount of times they attack at the end of turn is not always equal to your tentacle count because of the tentacle assembly effect or a cursed relic that make tentacles attack twice but lowers your tentacle cap
Tentacle assembly is gained from exalting and only lasts one turn realm mastery can affect the amount of tentacle assembly you can get per exalt as well
